title: ophrys scolopax subsp. attica
intro: Ophrys scolopax subsp. attica, also known as the bee orchid or Greek bee orchid, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Orchidaceae. It is native to Greece and has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its potential to lower blood pressure and reduce inflammation due to its flavonoid and phenolic acid content. The plant has been valued for its health benefits, which include its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, making it a potential remedy for various health issues. Ophrys scolopax subsp. attica can be prepared as an herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, although more research is needed to fully understand its effects and potential uses.
